title: Chinese Steel Exports Diverge: Seaborne Volumes Rise Despite Weak Customs Data
section_path: Chinese Steel Exports Diverge: Seaborne Volumes Rise Despite Weak Customs Data
section_path_text: Chinese Steel Exports Diverge: Seaborne Volumes Rise Despite Weak Customs Data
summary:
Main
Image reference: assets/2026-03-31_chinese-steel-exports-diverge-seaborne-volumes-rise-despite-weak-customs-data_img_rgb-color-brs-shipbrokers-monogram-l_c365f35f1de5.png
In early 2026, China's official customs data points to a sequential slowdown in steel exports, with cumulative shipments across January and February totalling 15.59mln mt, down 8.1% y-o-y. However, shipping data from AXSMarine presents a somewhat more nuanced picture. While reported export volumes have declined, shipments via dry bulk vessels have increased over the same period, rising by 12.3% y-o-y to 13.8mln mt.
